title: Cyclosorus moulmaynensis
taxonomy: Cyclosorus moulmaynensis is classified under the family Thelypteridaceae, which belongs to the order Polypodiales. This species is a type of fern, a group of vascular plants characterized by their leafy fronds. Within the Polypodiales order, Cyclosorus moulmaynensis is further classified as a member of the genus Cyclosorus. The classification of this species is also reflective of its evolutionary relationships with other ferns. The classification of Cyclosorus moulmaynensis is a reflection of its unique characteristics and its place within the fern family tree.
uses: Cyclosorus moulmaynensis is reportedly used in traditional medicine, particularly in the northeastern region of India. It is said to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Its leaves and roots are used in folk medicine to address different ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. The plant's medicinal uses are primarily based on traditional knowledge and may not be extensively scientifically validated.
